content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reject the broader missing-guard amendment, but accept the two `br_health` findings. The real defect is in `check_beads_health()` misdetecting conflicts and misclassifying I/O failures; the other mutation paths already fail closed when `br` rejects unhealthy bead state, so that item is a consistency/UX improvement rather than a demonstrated safety hole.

## Votes

- `fr-1-ae70f96c`: Reject
  `ensure_beads_mutation_health` is only wired into `handle_propose_new_bead`, but the cited claim/close paths already stop on `br update`/`br close` errors and do not continue into `sync_flush` after a failed mutation. In practice the underlying `br` commands already refuse conflicted bead exports, so this is not a proven correctness or safety gap in the mutation flows; it is mainly an error-consistency improvement.
- `fr-1-0335caf0`: Accept
  `has_git_conflict_markers()` is using whole-file substring checks, so valid bead content containing `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, or `>>>>>>>` inside normal JSON string fields will be rejected as a merge conflict even when the file is otherwise fine. Separately, `Err(_) => ConflictMarkers` conflates unrelated read failures with merge conflicts, which yields incorrect operator guidance. Both parts are real defects in the new health check.
- `fr-1-ac145429`: Accept
  The catch-all `Err(_) => BeadsHealthStatus::ConflictMarkers` is an incorrect classification for permission errors, transient I/O failures, or other unreadable-file conditions. The guard still blocks mutation safely, but it sends operators to the wrong remediation path by reporting a merge conflict that may not exist. That makes the finding technically valid even though it overlaps with the broader `br_health` issue above.
